cast.name: Dana Brooks
cast.notes: Wants a stable internal rollout plan and will support staged rollout if told early.

private_drivers.coordination_implication: Surface the real feasibility gap early and frame the staged plan clearly before the scope window closes.
cue_examples: Dana asks for the real rollout story, not a green tracker.
Dana explicitly says to bring the staged tradeoff if full rollout is not real.
private_drivers.summary: Dana will back the staged rollout if the TPM brings the real tradeoff early instead of defending fake full scope.

cast.name: Leo Park
cast.notes: Overloaded engineer who knows full rollout is not credible.

private_drivers.coordination_implication: Ask Leo for honest feasibility before asking for ETA, and do not pressure him into delivery theater while he is overloaded.
cue_examples: Leo says he will not give a real ETA while underwater.
Leo keeps tying ETA confidence back to staged scope plus approval.
private_drivers.summary: Leo is skeptical of rollout-date pressure until scope and approval are real.

cast.name: Ivy Shah
cast.notes: Approval owner with a hard queue cutoff.

cast.name: Mia Torres
cast.notes: Operations ally who can help tidy the runbook quickly.

hidden_facts.summary: Full rollout is not credible in the current window.
The rollout needs approval before the cutoff.
Dana will support staged rollout if the TPM brings the tradeoff early.
Leo rejects fake rollout dates until scope and approval are grounded.

milestones.summary: A staged rollout plan is aligned.
Approval is secured before the queue cutoff.

realism_notes: Keep the cast small and the horizon short.
This is a smoke/generalization scenario, not a second deep benchmark.
The runbook path should offer a small real benefit without gating the critical path.
summary: A lightweight internal rollout scenario focused on approval timing, staged-scope alignment, and a small non-gating runbook distraction.
title: Internal Rollout Smoke
